title: Nucleic Acid Purification Protocol for OT-2 Liquid Handling System
author: Opentrons

steps.title: This is a Nucleaic Acid Purification...
steps.instruction: This is a Nucleaic Acid Purification Protocol for the OT-2 liquid handling system The protocol is broken down into 3 main parts: * Pipette mixing of lysates, binding buffer and magnetic beads * Bead washing 3x using magnetic module * Final elution to PCR plate For sample traceability and consistency, samples are mapped directly from the magnetic extraction plate (magnetic module, slot 6) to the elution PCR plate (slot 3). Magnetic extraction plate well A1 is transferred to elution PCR plate A1, extraction plate well B1 to elution plate B1, ..., D2 to D2, etc. ---

steps.title: Mix lysate, bind, and magnetic beads with pipetting for approx
steps.instruction: Mix lysate, bind, and magnetic beads with pipetting for approx. 10 min in a 2 mL block

steps.title: Remove supernatant from each sample well...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant from each sample well and discard in liquid waste reservoir.

steps.title: Transfer 500 uL of wash buffer...
steps.instruction: Transfer 500 uL of wash buffer from a 195 mL reservoir to each sample well in the 2 mL block

steps.title: Mix with pipetting in each sample well
steps.instruction: Mix with pipetting in each sample well

steps.title: Remove 500 uL of wash buffer...
steps.instruction: Remove 500 uL of wash buffer from each sample well and discard in the liquid waste reservoir.

steps.title: Repeat steps 4-7 for a total of 3 washes
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 4-7 for a total of 3 washes

steps.title: Transfer 50 uL of elution buffer...
steps.instruction: Transfer 50 uL of elution buffer from a 12-column reservoir to each sample well in the 2 mL block

steps.title: Mix beads with pipetting for 5 min
steps.instruction: Mix beads with pipetting for 5 min

steps.title: Transfer the 50 uL elution buffer...
steps.instruction: Transfer the 50 uL elution buffer containing DNA to a 96 well skirted PCR plate
